summary: Reviewed the brand event-feed configuration and PDF/clipboard event-fetch workflow for failure handling, partial updates, retry/idempotency, and empty/error paths. The main risks are around partial brand updates preserving stale credentials or producing server errors instead of actionable validation, plus event feed failures silently degrading generated packs. Several external-call and empty-state paths are handled defensively.
appears_sound: `lib/eventFeed/index.ts` catches adapter failures and returns an empty event list, so PDF generation can continue instead of failing the whole pack when an event API is down.
`lib/eventFeed/baronshubAdapter.ts` uses `AbortSignal.timeout(10_000)` and checks `res.ok`, so BaronsHub requests have an explicit timeout and error branch.
`app/api/brands/route.ts` trims and length-checks `event_feed_api_key` before persistence, avoiding accidental whitespace-only keys and oversized secret values on create.
`components/brand/BrandForm.tsx` validates the create/edit flow before saving and surfaces API errors from brand save or logo upload rather than swallowing them.

findings.description: Update preserves any existing `event_feed_api_key` whenever the submitted key field is blank, even when the user disables the feed or switches providers. Failure sequence: user saves BaronsHub credentials, later selects `none` and saves, the key remains stored; when they re-enable another non-anchor feed without entering a fresh key, the old secret can be reused silently.
findings.evidence: `app/api/brands/[id]/route.ts:62` only assigns `dbInput.event_feed_api_key = rawApiKey` when `rawApiKey` is non-empty, while `BrandForm.tsx` sends `event_feed_type: "none"` with no key at `components/brand/BrandForm.tsx:164`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The product may intentionally retain secrets to make temporary feed disablement reversible, but the UI text only promises blank input preserves the key during normal edits, not when disabling or changing provider.
findings.what_would_confirm: Confirm the expected behavior for disabling/changing feed type; a test should assert whether `event_feed_api_key` is nulled when `event_feed_type` becomes `none` or the provider changes.

findings.description: The PUT route does not enforce complete config for non-anchor feeds before updating, so malformed edit requests can fall through to the database constraint and return 500. Failure sequence: user/API submits `event_feed_type: "baronshub"` without a base URL or key, Supabase rejects the row, and the UI sees a generic server failure instead of the actionable 400 validation used on create.
findings.evidence: `app/api/brands/[id]/route.ts:41` validates only a present URL and API-key length, while `app/api/brands/route.ts:48` explicitly returns 400 when non-anchor feeds lack base URL or key.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The current client blocks some missing-field cases, but direct API calls and stale `event_feed_has_key` states can still reach the route.
findings.what_would_confirm: Exercise PUT with `event_feed_type: "baronshub"` and missing `event_feed_base_url`/stored key; it should return 400 without relying on the DB CHECK constraint.

findings.description: Event feed failure during pack generation is silently converted into an empty event list, making the generated PDF/DOCX look successful but missing real upcoming events. Failure sequence: BaronsHub or Anchor times out/returns 500, `fetchEventsForBrand` returns `[]`, generation completes, and staff may print packs with placeholder or absent event promotion without knowing the integration failed.
findings.evidence: `lib/eventFeed/index.ts:43` catches all adapter errors and returns `[]`; `app/api/generate/route.ts:328` uses that result directly for the generated bundle.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Silent degradation may be intentional to preserve the core bingo generation workflow when optional event data is unavailable.
findings.what_would_confirm: Decide whether event feeds are optional or operator-visible; if visible failure is required, return a warning in the bundle response or include an explicit placeholder stating the feed fetch failed.

findings.description: The Anchor adapter likely lacks the timeout protection added to BaronsHub, so a slow Anchor Management API can stall the entire generate request. Failure sequence: user clicks Generate, the Anchor API hangs, and the request waits until platform/server timeout instead of quickly degrading to an empty event list.
findings.evidence: `lib/eventFeed/baronshubAdapter.ts:168` shows `signal: AbortSignal.timeout(10_000)`, but the included Anchor adapter excerpt and comments do not show an equivalent timeout branch.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The pack truncates `lib/eventFeed/anchorAdapter.ts`, so a timeout may exist later in the omitted fetch code.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the fetch call inside `createAnchorAdapter(...).fetchUpcomingEvents`; if it lacks an abort signal, add the same bounded timeout used by BaronsHub.

empty_categories: No duplicate-submit double-create issue was confirmed from the pack because brand creation/update is single-request and DB constraints are the main integrity boundary shown.
No empty-event crash found in PDF/DOCX consumers; `lib/clipboardDocx.ts` explicitly handles missing events with a placeholder paragraph.
No unmount-after-async React state update issue was material enough to flag from the changed form code.
No file-upload cleanup regression was introduced by the event-feed changes in the provided pack.
